title: OSMM Offer Object
description: The intended value exchange extended to an audience in exchange for a call to action — a time-bound, promotional incentive to act (financial offer, free trial, demo, discount, bundle, BOGO, etc.). It carries the behavior-change objective and incentive philosophy (folded from the former Offer Strategy object) and references the Product Context it promotes rather than restating product features. Canonical schema for object_type 'offer'. The osmm-offer-builder skill references this file; it is the single source of truth for the object's shape.

osmm_version.description: OSMM schema version this instance conforms to (semantic version).

offer_id.description: Stable, human-readable id. Prefix 'OFR-' + lowercase hyphen slug. Never changes once assigned.

version.description: Instance version (e.g. '1.0'). Bump on revision; the id stays stable.

name.description: The offer's go-to-market name (e.g. '$5 Biggie Bag', 'watsonx 30-Day Free Trial').

offer_type.description: Governed offer-type vocabulary. Pick the single closest value.

description.description: 2-3 neutral, factual sentences: what the offer is, who it's for, and the action it asks for.

value_exchange.description: What the customer gets in return for taking the action.

call_to_action.description: The action requested of the customer (e.g. 'Start a free trial', 'Book a demo', 'Order in the app').

incentive_structure.description: The mechanics of the incentive (e.g. '30 days free, no card required', '$5 for a 4-item meal', '0% APR for 60 months').

behavior_change_objective.description: The desired behavior change this offer drives (folded from the former Offer Strategy object).

strategic_rationale.description: Incentive philosophy / why this offer is the right value exchange (folded from the former Offer Strategy object). Optional.

eligibility.description: Who qualifies / inclusion rules for the offer. Optional.

redemption.description: How the offer is redeemed or fulfilled. Optional.

economics.description: Directional offer economics — not precise finance. Optional.

cost_basis.description: What the offer costs to extend, directionally (e.g. 'discounted margin on a 4-item meal').

profitability_threshold.description: The directional break-even / profitability bar (e.g. 'profitable above N redemptions or with attach of a drink upsell').

margin_notes.description: Directional notes on margin impact, subsidy, or LTV rationale.

positioning.description: How the offer is framed to the audience (the angle the deal is sold on). Optional.

terms_conditions.description: Key terms, restrictions, or limitations on the offer. Optional.

availability.description: The time-bound window and channels the offer runs in. Optional.

start.description: When the offer becomes available (date or plain-language).

end.description: When the offer ends (date or plain-language).

duration.description: How long the offer runs (e.g. 'limited-time', '30 days', 'ongoing while in market').

channels.description: Channels the offer is available through (e.g. app, web, in-store).

linked_product.description: Id of the Product Context this offer promotes. Use 'PRD-PLACEHOLDER-<slug>' until it exists. Optional.

linked_audiences.description: Ids of the Audiences the offer is for. 'AUD-PLACEHOLDER-<slug>' ok. Optional.

linked_business_context.description: Id of the Business Context that extends the offer. 'BIZ-PLACEHOLDER-<slug>' ok. Optional.

source.description: One line: what source(s) this was built from and approximate date. Optional.
